title: Watch This Cyborg Cockroach Test Its New Diving Suit
gist: Nanyang Technological University engineers built a 3-D-printed underwater breathing apparatus for remote-controlled cyborg cockroaches, extending the biohybrid search-and-rescue robots already field-tested after Myanmar's 2025 earthquake.

insights.claim: After a 7.7-magnitude earthquake struck Myanmar in 2025, remote-controlled cyborg Madagascar hissing cockroaches, guided via tiny silver electrodes, were deployed to search rubble for survivors.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Demonstrates real-world field use of insect cyborgs in disaster response.

insights.claim: Nanyang Technological University engineers built a 3-D-printed SCUBA suit, reported in Nature Communications, that lets cyborg cockroaches breathe underwater via an oxygen tank, tubes, and a waterproof shell.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Extends biohybrid robots into aquatic disaster environments.

insights.claim: The suit generates oxygen chemically using a manganese-dioxide-coated sponge that catalyzes production from hydrogen peroxide, delivered through silicone tubes glued to the insect's spiracles.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Novel compact chemical oxygen supply for micro-scale biohybrids.
